continents.name: Cauldenmoor
continents.description: The most populous continent. Most diverse terrain, most powers, most conflict. Fertile river valleys between low forested hills, navigable waterways. The world's most contested real estate.

powers.name: Aldenmere
powers.type: Old feudal kingdom, powerful military tradition

powers.name: The Kingdom of Durn
powers.type: Old feudal kingdom, powerful military tradition

powers.name: The Ironmark Borderlands
powers.type: Contested zone between Durn and the Ironmark
powers.notes: Starting region for certain character backgrounds

continents.name: Valdenmere
continents.description: Cold, northern, heavily forested. Oldest surviving civilizations. Deeply conservative. Short summers, long winters. Dark conifer forest broken by granite ridgelines.

powers.name: Thornsveld
powers.current_leader: King Aldric IV, 61, no legitimate heir

powers.name: The Karn Clans
powers.current_notable: Clan-chief Halvard Ironmark — hasn't raided in four years, considered reasonable

continents.name: Ossrath
continents.description: Dry interior plateau, ancient ruins of the Varathi Collapse, contested and dangerous. High elevation, thin air cold at night, burning days. The ruins are everywhere — normalized by locals, disturbing to outsiders.

powers.name: The Salvage Authority
powers.stability: moderate — control fades 30 miles from settlements

powers.name: The Drift Lords
powers.stability: stable internally, officially ignored by Authority

powers.name: Keth
powers.stability: politically tense — Dust Council succession crisis
powers.notes: Starting region anchor. Governed by seven-seat Merchant Oligarchy called the Dust Council.

powers.name: The True Reckoning
powers.type: Military occupier of ruins, claims Varathi succession

continents.name: Solvane
continents.description: Southern, coastal, warmest continent. Highest urban density. Most trade-oriented. Nearly year-round growing season. Amber-gold coastal waters, white stone cliffs, sheltered harbors. The gap between wealthy and poor is enormous and visible.

powers.name: The Solvanic Dominion
powers.type: Nominally hereditary monarchy, functions as managed oligarchy of noble trading houses
powers.stability: stable but queen is secretly ill
powers.current_leader: Queen Aldeth Calmaveth III, 47, ill — not publicly known

continents.name: Drenmark
continents.description: Northwestern continent. Wet, dark forests, moor and fen. Oldest religious traditions. Largest unbroken wilderness on Arneth in the Free Fens region. Peat, bog, ancient trees. Animist faith deep-rooted.

powers.name: The Drenmari See
powers.type: Religious authority — politically weak but culturally deep
powers.stability: stable, de facto governance through temple-settlements

powers.name: Ashenveld
powers.notes: Nominally claims the Free Fens. Ignored.

powers.name: The Free Fens
powers.type: De facto independent region — no formal state, governed by village councils and informal Fen Lords council
powers.stability: stable internally, resistant to external authority

powers.name: Mossward
powers.type: Largest Free Fens settlement — informal capital

continents.name: The Shattered Reach
continents.description: Archipelago continent — dozens of large islands. Remnants of the Drowned Centuries. Maritime culture. The light is extraordinary. Nights glow with bioluminescent harbor water. Every language, every faith present in the port cities.

powers.name: The Reach Compact
powers.stability: stable but checked by Corsair Throne

powers.name: The Corsair Throne
powers.type: Pirate sovereign — technically recognized, formally contested
powers.current_leader: The Iron Wake (Rael Sorveth, 54, former Reach Compact naval officer)
powers.notes: Controls enough sea lanes to veto Compact decisions. Pressing for formal recognition within Compact structure. Starting region anchor (Tidewarden Anchorage).

no_mans_lands.description: Due to the sheer enormity of Arneth's six continents, many major powers do not border each other. Between them are vast uncontrolled territories — dangerous, free, and slowly filling back in.
no_mans_lands.character: Desperate homesteaders, bandit confederations, minor lords trying to carve out something new. The most dangerous and most free places in the world.
contents: Former war zones reverting to forest and scrub
Ruins of settlements that died in the Turnings
Drift Lord and nomadic confederation territory

coalition_note: 42 powers across 6 continents creates enormous potential for coalitions. Many powers do not share borders — alliances form around trade interests, religious affiliation, shared enemies, and Hegemony-era inherited relationships.
